title: Exolepta calyculata

taxonomy: Exolepta calyculata belongs to the family Pteromalidae, which is a group of wasps within the order Hymenoptera. It is a type of chalcid wasp that is known for its parasitic behavior. The classification of Exolepta calyculata also includes the order of insects, class Insecta, and kingdom Animalia. This insect's classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other organisms. Further, its classification also aids in identifying its ecological role in the ecosystem.
benefits: Exolepta calyculata, also known as the thorny head beetle, is often found associated with the plant Lantana camara. Although little is known about Exolepta calyculata itself, information about its associated plant has some health benefits. Lantana camara has been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases.
